sae_features.label: Membrane protein luminal ectodomains
sae_features.description: Non‑cytosolic ectodomains/luminal loops of secretory‑pathway membrane proteins: long, structured regions exposed to the ER lumen or extracellular space (often glycosylated and disulfide‑bonded), while excluding signal peptides, transmembrane helices, and cytosolic segments

sae_features.label: Extracellular mucin-like PTS segments
sae_features.description: Extracellular mucin-like PTS segments: long Ser/Thr/Pro-rich, low‑complexity, disordered stalk/linker regions prone to dense mucin‑type O‑glycosylation, typically located just after the signal peptide or immediately N‑terminal to a transmembrane helix or structured ectodomain in cell‑surface and viral glycoproteins.

sae_features.label: N-terminal ectodomain recognition module
sae_features.description: N-terminal ectodomain recognition module in secreted, GPI-anchored, and membrane glycoproteins (type I, type II, or multi-pass): the first folded extracellular domain (or N-terminal half of a large ectodomain) immediately after the signal peptide or luminal signal-anchor, typically glycosylated and disulfide-stabilized, that mediates ligand binding, adhesion, receptor engagement, or supramolecular assembly; fold-agnostic (observed across ZP-N, Sema, FN3, MHC α1-like, LAMP luminal, adhesion GPCR NTF, and mosaic domains of type II proteases) and largely inactive on transmembranes/cytosolic regions and more distal ectodomain modules.

sae_features.label: Repeat-rich glycosylated ectodomains
sae_features.description: Extracellular/luminal ectodomains of secreted and membrane glycoproteins with large N-terminal domains—predominantly type I single-pass receptors (plant RLKs; animal adhesion/immune and growth factor receptors), but also GPI-anchored receptor-like proteins and multi-pass proteins with substantial ectodomains (e.g., adhesion GPCRs, DOMON–cytochrome b561 fusions)—characterized by modular repeat domains (LRR, lectin/LysM, EGF, Ig/FNIII, cadherin) interspersed with long low-complexity Ser/Thr/Pro-rich, glycosylation-prone and cysteine-rich segments

sae_features.label: Juxtamembrane ectodomain stalk
sae_features.description: Extracellular juxtamembrane “stalk/ectodomain stem” immediately N‑terminal to a transmembrane helix in metazoan cell‑surface glycoproteins (and analogous luminal loops before TMs in multipass proteins). These segments are typically Pro/Ser/Thr/Gly- and acidic‑rich, often low‑complexity or flexible with GP/PG clusters but may include short amphipathic helices; they frequently harbor N-/O‑glycosylation and protease shedding/processing sites. When an EGF‑ or SEA‑like module abuts the membrane, the feature extends into the C‑terminal part of that module.

sae_features.label: Signal peptide cleavage-site detector
sae_features.description: Recognition of the N‑terminal signal peptide—specifically the hydrophobic h‑region and its C‑terminal boundary (h→c transition) around the signal peptidase cleavage site—in secreted and secretory‑pathway proteins (including soluble factors and single‑pass receptors)

sae_features.label: TM-flanking low-complexity IDRs
sae_features.description: Low‑complexity, Ser/Thr/Pro–rich intrinsically disordered segments that flank signal peptides or transmembrane helices in secretory‑pathway and cell‑surface proteins—i.e., mucin‑like lumenal ectodomains and glycan‑attachment tracts—and, by extension, analogous proline/polar/charged disordered cytosolic tails; the feature avoids transmembrane helices and well‑folded catalytic domains.

sae_features.label: Helix-bundle interaction modules
sae_features.description: Alpha‑helical bundle protein–protein interaction modules that mediate dimerization/oligomerization and scaffolding—most prominently death‑fold prodomains (DD/DED/CARD/PYD) but extending to other helix‑rich dimerization domains (e.g., TRFH) and extracellular helix‑rich ectodomains (e.g., four‑helix cytokines, gamete‑fusion ectodomains)
